title: Economic Bulletin Issue 7, 2025
summary: The ECB Governing Council held rates unchanged at its October 30, 2025 meeting, with inflation at 2.2% and the economy growing 0.2% in Q3 2025. The ECB reiterated its data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach without pre-committing to a rate path, citing ongoing uncertainty from global trade disputes and geopolitical tensions.
key_points: ECB Governing Council kept three key interest rates unchanged on October 30, 2025
Euro area inflation rose to 2.2% in September 2025 from 2.0% in August, driven by energy prices (-0.4% vs -2.0% prior)
GDP growth was 0.2% in Q3 2025 per Eurostat flash estimate; services grew while manufacturing was held back by tariffs and uncertainty
Unemployment remained at 6.3% in September, near historical lows
Bank lending rates for firms averaged 3.5% in August; corporate bond issuance growth slowed to 3.3% yearly
ECB decided to advance the digital euro project to the next stage for technical readiness
Forward guidance: ECB will base rate decisions on inflation outlook assessment, incoming data, underlying inflation dynamics, and monetary policy transmission strength
why_it_matters: This ECB Economic Bulletin provides the official institutional assessment of euro area economic conditions and policy intentions. For macro traders and researchers, it offers the ECB's characterization of growth trajectory, inflation dynamics, and forward guidance language that shapes rate expectations. The bulletin signals no urgency to change policy while acknowledging downside risks from trade tensions and geopolitical uncertainty.
macro_context: The bulletin describes a euro area economy with modest 0.2% Q3 growth, elevated but stabilizing inflation (2.2%), and resilient labor markets (6.3% unemployment). Services sector shows strength from tourism and AI-related IT investment, while manufacturing faces headwinds from tariffs and export weakness. The ECB characterizes the policy stance as appropriate given data-dependent assessment without signaling near-term rate changes.
important_numbers: 3.5% average bank lending rate for firms (August 2025)
2.9% annual growth rate of bank lending to firms (September 2025)
